title: Customer Tiered Rollout Sequence
description: A release-and-rollout control workflow that manages promotion waves, rollback readiness, and deployment dependencies across environments. Focus case: customer tiered rollout sequence.
assumptions: Release instances are single authoritative workflow objects representing a promo sequence.
Customer tiers and cohorts are pre-registered and represented as available resources prior to assignment.
Automation jobs and test suites execute deterministically and their availability is represented by boolean facts.
risks: Incorrect binding order (e.g., promoting before dependency or canary policy binding) can leave releases in an inconsistent state.
Manual approvals represented as consumable tokens may be lost or consumed prematurely, blocking progression.
Tight coupling of multiple validation artifacts increases choreography complexity and risk of deadlock if one artifact is unavailable.
